questions.q: Which physical condition did Dhritarashtra have from birth?
questions.explanation: Dhritarashtra was born blind, which is why he is remembered as the Blind King of Hastinapura.

questions.q: Who was Dhritarashtra's wife?
questions.explanation: Dhritarashtra married Gandhari, the princess of Gandhara, who blindfolded herself for life.

questions.q: By what collective name were Dhritarashtra's hundred sons known?
questions.explanation: His hundred sons were called the Kauravas, after their ancestor King Kuru.

questions.q: Who was Dhritarashtra's eldest son?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana was the eldest of the hundred sons and the driving force behind the war.

questions.q: Which younger brother of Dhritarashtra was crowned king of Hastinapura?
questions.explanation: Pandu, his pale younger brother, was crowned king while Dhritarashtra was passed over.

questions.q: Which sage was Dhritarashtra's biological father?
questions.explanation: Vyasa fathered Dhritarashtra through niyoga when Vichitravirya died childless.

questions.q: From which capital city did Dhritarashtra rule?
questions.explanation: Dhritarashtra ruled from Hastinapura, the ancestral City of Elephants on the Ganga.

questions.q: Although he was the elder brother, why was Dhritarashtra initially denied the crown?
options: He was born to a serving maid
He was cursed by a sage
questions.explanation: His blindness was held to disqualify him from kingship, so the throne went to Pandu.

questions.q: Who narrated the events of the Kurukshetra war to Dhritarashtra as they happened?
questions.explanation: Sanjaya, his charioteer, reported the entire war to the blind king using divine sight.

questions.q: Who was Dhritarashtra's mother?
questions.explanation: Ambika, widow of Vichitravirya, bore Dhritarashtra through the niyoga with Vyasa.

questions.q: According to the epic, why was Dhritarashtra born blind?
options: A sage cursed his mother during pregnancy
He was injured during a difficult birth
He inherited the condition from Vichitravirya
Ambika shut her eyes when she saw Vyasa
questions.explanation: Frightened by Vyasa's fierce appearance, Ambika closed her eyes during their union, so her son was born blind.

questions.q: After the war, what did the grieving Dhritarashtra crush in his powerful embrace?
options: A stone pillar of the court
questions.explanation: Intending to kill Bhima, he crushed an iron statue of him that had been pushed forward in Bhima's place.

questions.q: Who saved Bhima by substituting the iron statue for him in Dhritarashtra's embrace?
questions.explanation: Krishna, sensing the old king's murderous grief, pushed the iron statue into his arms instead of Bhima.

questions.q: Yuyutsu, the only son of Dhritarashtra to survive the war, was born to which mother?
questions.explanation: Yuyutsu was Dhritarashtra's son by a vaishya serving woman, and he crossed over to the Pandava side before the war.

questions.q: What was the name of Dhritarashtra's only daughter?
questions.explanation: Duhshala was his only daughter, later married to Jayadratha, king of Sindhu.

questions.q: Terrified by evil omens after Draupadi's humiliation in the dice hall, what did Dhritarashtra do?
options: He ordered a second game at once
He abdicated in favor of Yudhishthira
He granted Draupadi boons that freed the Pandavas
questions.explanation: Frightened by the omens, he offered Draupadi boons, by which she freed her husbands and restored their wealth.

questions.q: What advice did the wise, including Vidura, give Dhritarashtra at Duryodhana's ill-omened birth?
options: Name him Suyodhana to ward off the omens
Dedicate the boy to temple service
Abandon the inauspicious child to save the dynasty
Send him to Gandhara to be raised
questions.explanation: Jackals howled at the birth, and the wise urged the king to abandon the child for the good of the line, but he refused.

questions.q: Who granted Sanjaya the divine sight that let him report the war to Dhritarashtra?
questions.explanation: Vyasa offered the king sight to watch the war, but Dhritarashtra declined, so Vyasa gave divine vision to Sanjaya instead.

questions.q: During the game of dice, which eager question did Dhritarashtra keep repeating, betraying his greed?
questions.explanation: As each stake fell, the blind king repeatedly asked whether it had been won, revealing his complicity in the game.

questions.q: Roughly how many years did Dhritarashtra live under Yudhishthira's rule before retiring to the forest?
questions.explanation: The Ashramavasika Parva records that he stayed about fifteen years in Hastinapura before taking up forest life.

questions.q: How did Dhritarashtra die?
options: In a forest fire he refused to flee
Drowned while bathing in the Ganga
Peacefully in his sleep at Hastinapura
Slain in the night raid on the camp
questions.explanation: A wildfire swept his forest retreat, and the old king sat facing it in meditation rather than flee.

questions.q: Which half-brother and counselor repeatedly urged Dhritarashtra to restrain Duryodhana?
questions.explanation: Vidura, his wise half-brother, warned him again and again to curb Duryodhana, advice the king never followed.

questions.q: What does the name Dhritarashtra literally mean?
options: One who sees with the mind
questions.explanation: The name combines dhrita, held firm, with rashtra, nation, meaning he who holds or supports the kingdom.

questions.q: In the Ashramavasika Parva, what miraculous vision did Vyasa grant Dhritarashtra and the other elders by the Ganga?
options: The dead warriors of Kurukshetra rising from the river for one night
A preview of the evils of the Kali Yuga
questions.explanation: Vyasa summoned the slain warriors from the Ganga for a single night so the mourners could see their loved ones.

questions.q: Who accompanied Dhritarashtra when he retired to the forest?
questions.explanation: Gandhari, Kunti, Vidura, and Sanjaya all followed the old king into forest retirement.

questions.q: Why did Dhritarashtra refuse to flee the forest fire that killed him?
options: He was trapped by fallen trees
He believed Vyasa would save him
Emaciated by fasting, he accepted the fire as a sacred death
He wished to die where his sons had fallen
questions.explanation: Weakened by severe austerities, he chose to sit in meditation and accept death by fire as a fitting end.

questions.q: What was the source of the forest fire that consumed Dhritarashtra?
options: It spread from the hermits' own abandoned sacrificial fires
A blaze set by careless hunters
The lingering heat of Ashwatthama's weapon
questions.explanation: Narada reports in the Ashramavasika Parva that the blaze sprang from the old king's own sacred ritual fires, which his priests had left burning in the forest.

questions.q: Despite his blindness, for which extraordinary attribute was Dhritarashtra famed?
options: The strength of ten thousand elephants
The ability to foresee the future
questions.explanation: The epic credits him with the strength of ten thousand elephants, which is how he crushed the iron statue of Bhima.

questions.q: Who escaped the forest fire at Dhritarashtra's own urging?
questions.explanation: Dhritarashtra commanded Sanjaya to save himself, and the charioteer alone escaped and departed for the Himalayas.

questions.q: In the Adi Parva, Dhritarashtra recalls the war in a long lament whose verses end with which refrain?
options: Then I wept for my hundred sons
Then I lost hope of victory
questions.explanation: Recounting each calamity to Sanjaya, the king repeats that at each event he lost all hope of victory.
